title: betula alba subsp. pubescens
intro: Betula alba subsp. pubescens, commonly known as the downy birch, is a subspecies of the white birch tree (Betula alba) native to Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, particularly salicin, a glycoside similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Betula alba subsp. pubescens include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as treating fever and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and other remedies for various health issues.
